preliminary.explanation_en: happening before the main part of something
preliminary.example_sentence: We had a preliminary meeting to discuss the project goals.
preliminary.original_sentence: When an architect gets an idea for a new building, he makes a preliminary drawing.

storyboard.explanation_en: a series of drawings that show how a film or commercial will look
storyboard.example_sentence: The director showed the storyboard to the team before filming started.
storyboard.original_sentence: When a creative advertising person gets an idea for a new TV commercial, he puts it into storyboard form, a series of drawings that suggest what the idea will look like in finished form.

first draft.explanation_en: the first version of a piece of writing
first draft.example_sentence: I finished the first draft of my essay last night.
first draft.original_sentence: Writers with idea prepare a first draft.

shape up.explanation_en: to organize or improve something to make it ready
shape up.example_sentence: You need to shape up your notes before the presentation.
shape up.original_sentence: NOTE Shape up the idea on paper.

tangible.explanation_en: clear enough to be touched or clearly seen
tangible.example_sentence: We need tangible evidence before we can make a decision.
tangible.original_sentence: When the idea takes tangible form, you can literally look at it, see the loopholes, see what it needs in the way of polish.

loopholes.explanation_en: small mistakes or gaps in a plan or rule
loopholes.example_sentence: The lawyer found several loopholes in the contract.
loopholes.original_sentence: When the idea takes tangible form, you can literally look at it, see the loopholes, see what it needs in the way of polish.

polish.explanation_en: the act of improving something to make it perfect
polish.example_sentence: The article is good, but it needs a little more polish.
polish.original_sentence: When the idea takes tangible form, you can literally look at it, see the loopholes, see what it needs in the way of polish.

oral.example_sentence: The exam consists of both a written test and an oral presentation.
oral.original_sentence: The first salesman gave me strictly an oral presentation.

technical details.explanation_en: specific, complicated information about a subject
technical details.example_sentence: I don't need all the technical details, just tell me how it works.
technical details.original_sentence: He brought in taxes, options, social security, all the technical details of insurance programming.

approach.explanation_en: a way of doing or thinking about something
approach.example_sentence: We need a new approach to solve this problem.
approach.original_sentence: The second salesman used a different approach.

grasp.example_sentence: It took me a while to grasp the main idea of the book.
grasp.original_sentence: I could grasp his proposal easily and quickly because I could literally see it.

resolve.explanation_en: to make a firm decision to do something
resolve.example_sentence: I resolve to exercise more this year.
resolve.original_sentence: Resolve to put your ideas in saleable form.

saleable.explanation_en: good enough to be sold or accepted by others
saleable.example_sentence: The designer made the product more saleable by changing the color.
saleable.original_sentence: Resolve to put your ideas in saleable form.
